title: ctenitis hypolepioides
intro: Ctenitis hypolepioides, also known as Hypolepis crenata, is a species of fern in the family Dicksoniaceae, native to New Zealand and Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ctenitis hypolepioides include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a digestive aid. Herbal preparations of Ctenitis hypolepioides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or extracting their active compounds with a solvent.
